attacking_style: Versatile aggressor who attacks from long range (10km+ solo breaks) when in exceptional form, or contests uphill sprints from reduced bunches. Rarely waits for final 200m, preferring to impose from distance.
team_tactics: Jumbo-Visma sets pace on climbs/finale to control then deliver him (7+ instances), or occupies multiple front positions (E3 pattern). Can win isolated when leadout fails. Often sacrifices own chances for GC leaders mid-race.
terrain_preference: Thrives on uphill sprint finishes, rolling courses after climbs shed pure sprinters, and long time trials (40km+). Crosswind selections favor him. Mountains vulnerable only at HC gradients beyond 3.5km.
vulnerability: Poor positioning in pure flat sprints (boxed in repeatedly 2022-23). Struggles after mechanicals on climbs. Crosswind echelon selections can split him from group (Omloop 2020). Form inconsistent at Tour starts.
key_patterns: Won stages across three terrain types (mountain, TT, sprint) in single Tour, twice (2021-22)
Frequently boxed in flat bunch sprints despite team control, tactical positioning weakness (4 instances)
Executes 10km+ solo attacks while wearing race leader jersey, exceptional confidence pattern
Leads climbs as domestique then contests finish, dual role within stages (5+ instances)
Dominates long TTs 30-40km at 51kph+ average speed in third week (3 wins)
Two-rider Jumbo breakaways with Laporte split world-class fields repeatedly (E3, Dauphine patterns)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Tour showed clear decline with positioning errors, dropped on stages, no sprint competitiveness versus strong 2022 dominance pattern
